checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: Created docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md with canonical provider ID table, stability contract (immutable, never reassigned), deprecation policy (5-step process), changelog expectation. Linked from README.md and docs/API.md. No code changes. Minor deviation: acceptance criteria called for deprecation policy covering 'renames with both old and new IDs served concurrently'; the delivered doc takes an 'immutable IDs' stance and frames deprecation around provider unavailability rather than renames. The mechanism (deprecated ID stays valid on read paths, new ID can be added) effectively supports the same outcome.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only 3 documentation files changed: new docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, one-line link addition in README.md, small 'See Also' section in docs/API.md. No source code changes, no unrelated churn. Exactly matches the plan's scope.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: Documentation is substantive (~63 lines, well-structured with 5 sections including a full provider table). No placeholder/empty text. No tests were modified — verify command passed on a docs-only diff. Real, actionable content for integrators.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: Documentation is a policy statement, not executable code. No security or correctness risk. Links are valid relative paths. Deprecation behavior described (400 with provider_deprecated error code) is reasonable. No misleading or dangerous claims.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence
